items.source: Avoma 2026-05-08 action items; no confirming Next Step entry since

metrics.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre detailed the operational pain in depth on the 8 Jan 2026 discovery call (~6 months ago): roughly 100 active vendors onboarded via emailed PDF forms stored in SharePoint, invoices processed manually from a shared AP inbox with approval routed to the COO, and payments executed by manually uploading files to BMO's online banking platform. The 8 May 2026 demo (~9 weeks ago) reiterated the same need to 'automate supplier onboarding with approval workflows, reduce manual invoice entry effort, and streamline cross-border payment processing.' No dollar-quantified savings or ROI target has been voiced by the buyer; the only numbers on the table are Zycus's own indicative pricing ($80K-150K annual license plus a roughly 1:1 implementation fee, quoted 8 Jan 2026). Status: partial, pain is vivid and specific but a buyer-owned business case with numbers has not been built.

champion.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre has been the connective tissue of this deal since the 8 Jan 2026 discovery call: he coordinated requirements-gathering, arranged the 8 May 2026 demo, and kept the Next Step log updated through 26 Jun 2026. His own framing on 8 Jan 2026 ('my task is... to help demo based on high-level requirements... to our finance team') reads as a facilitator role rather than a Zycus advocate arguing internally against alternatives. He has been on vacation since 19 Jun 2026 (~3 weeks), and the deal has fully stalled in that window with no alternate contact picking up the thread. Status: developing, real engagement but unproven advocacy and currently a single point of failure.

competition.narrative: Salesforce lists a broad competitor field (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, Ivalua, JAGGAER, Oracle, SAP Ariba, Workday, Ironclad), but on the 8 Jan 2026 call the buyer explicitly declined to name which vendors it was evaluating, saying only that some are 'on the Gartner... list' and others are 'smaller scale.' No fresher competitive signal has surfaced since. Status: partial, a real multi-vendor evaluation is confirmed to exist, but which rivals are actually live and Zycus's standing among them is unconfirmed.

identify_pain.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre gave a detailed, first-hand walkthrough of the pain on 8 Jan 2026: vendor onboarding via emailed PDF forms filed in SharePoint with no central system of record, no purchase-order workflow ('strictly invoice-driven'), and payments made by manually building and uploading files to BMO's online banking portal. The 8 May 2026 demo corroborated the same picture from the AP side: invoices arrive in a shared Outlook mailbox, are manually downloaded to SharePoint, validated against contracts, then routed for approval and coded into QuickBooks Online. Status: confirmed, consistent and specific across two calls six months apart.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, Order Form, or any contracting artifact has been referenced in any source read for this opportunity; the deal remains in Formal Evaluation, still short of commercial agreement. The 8 May 2026 demo closed only with a commitment to exchange a commercial-calculation form, not paper. Status: gap, appropriate for this stage, but notable given the close date is only 21 days away.

economic_buyer.narrative: On the 8 Jan 2026 call, Heinrich Zaguirre stated that invoice payment approvals ultimately go to 'our COO' for sign-off on the amount, but never named this person, and no COO has joined a call in the six months since. Salesforce lists Jennifer Newman as the account's Chief Operating Officer, but no call, email, or task in this read links her to this opportunity, so the connection is unconfirmed. The two most senior buyer participants engaged to date, Anthony Fonzo (Director, Finance & Operations) and Denni Wong (Director, Information Technology), were named on the 8 May 2026 demo as the deal's evaluators but sit below the budget-approval layer Heinrich described. Status: gap, the economic buyer is neither confidently named nor engaged.

decision_process.narrative: Heinrich Zaguirre (IT) has run point on scheduling and requirements-gathering throughout (sending requirements to Shifa Garewal on 16 Mar 2026, coordinating the 8 May 2026 demo), while Anthony Fonzo and Denni Wong were identified on that demo as the two people 'evaluating payment integration and overall solution.' Heinrich described a two-step process on 8 Jan 2026: gather AP-team requirements, then hold a tailored demo, with an unnamed COO approving budget at the end. No dated decision milestone has been set since the 8 May 2026 demo. Status: partial, the players are known but no forward-dated decision gate exists.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation centers on three components the buyer itself named on 8 Jan 2026: vendor/supplier management (~100 active vendors), invoice management (AP automation), and payment management (cross-border execution via TransferMate). The 8 May 2026 demo confirmed the same three-part scope plus specific asks around configurable approval rules, data validation on onboarding fields, and Microsoft Teams-embedded workflows. No formal RFP, BRD, or ShoeFit document has surfaced in any source read. Status: partial, real buyer-articulated criteria exist but are not yet formalized in writing.

value.how_we_win: Position single-platform, organically-built architecture, Merlin AI maturity, and total cost of ownership against Coupa's scale narrative (unconfirmed as a live rival in this deal).

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Michael McCarthy (Zycus VP Sales) 'previously spent 4 years at Coupa and 20+ years in tech at Oracle, positioning Zycus against those platforms' (Avoma demo notes 2026-05-08). Heinrich confirmed vendors 'on the Gartner quadrant' are being evaluated (2026-01-08 discovery call).
value.source: Avoma demo call 2026-05-08 notes; 2026-01-08 discovery call (Heinrich); Competitors__c
value.how_we_win: Neutralize Coupa's native payment advantage by pivoting to bank-file-only integration (acceptable per Heinrich) or alternative payment partner, then anchor win on Zycus's Merlin AI invoice OCR + Teams integration + AP Smartdesk auto-classification strengths shown in 2026-05-08 demo—capabilities Coupa does not match at this price point.

value.name: Coupa
value.quote: Heinrich stated 2026-01-08: 'there will be also on the Gartner list'; Coupa is a Gartner MQ leader in S2P and listed in SF Competitors__c.
value.how_we_win: Leverage Michael McCarthy's 4 years at Coupa to counter their narrative; position Zycus as faster implementation (2-3 months vs Coupa's typical 6-12), lower cost (estimated $80-150k annual vs Coupa's $200k+ for similar scope), and stronger AI/Merlin differentiation (Teams integration, embedded chat assistant, OCR learning).
